title: Stint-embargo CV with strictly OOF encodings and stacks
summary: Purged and embargoed CV exists precisely because overlapping sequential samples can leak signal across folds.
rationale: Purged and embargoed CV exists precisely because overlapping sequential samples can leak signal across folds. Here, adjacent laps from the same driver-race-stint are near-duplicates, so even race-aware CV can still look too optimistic when custom encodings, pit-window priors, or stacked experts are built on all training rows.
implementation_hint: When evaluating a fold, embargo neighboring laps or whole adjacent stint blocks for the same driver-race around the validation segment, and rebuild all frequency/target encodings and stacker inputs strictly out-of-fold under that splitter. Use this as the selection CV for new experts rather than only the easier grouped split.
expected_effect: Improves correlation between local CV and leaderboard behavior and filters out ideas that only exploit overlap leakage.
risk: This may lower local CV immediately and reduces effective sample size; it is mainly a model-selection improvement rather than a direct feature gain.
